betterquesting:10.desc:8:
I am quite impressed that it took me this long, living as a cosmic being, to find another fellow scientist outside of Earth. I do not exclude some sort of selection bias here, but from what I am seeing, magic is by far the easiest way to go interplanetary as a species.
What is good about this situation is that I feel less alone. There are others, ouside of the Arbiter, who have ascended through science and engineering.
What is bad about this situation is, well, everything else. Literally.
The scientists I encountered in Gallifrey are, well were, botanists. Botanists of extreme prowess, mapping how vegetal life spreads and evolves throughout the universe.
Here in Gallifrey, they have decided to quarantine themselves to study a most peculiar, to their eyes, form of life. Growing flesh. Flesh that grows into trees, with fruits.
Flesh that can take over its surroundings incredibly quickly.
Flesh that can bring forth undead creatures.
Flesh that is controlled by the Meatball Man.
